---
title: "MaAsLiN3 Loop Workflow"
author: "Elad Deiss-Yehiely"
date: "2026-03-31"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
###############################################################################
#  MaAsLiN3 — Multi-Outcome Loop
#  Loops over multiple primary outcomes, runs TSS and CLR for each,
#  collects everything into one master dataframe, and saves to disk.
###############################################################################
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 0.  PACKAGES & SETUP
# ===========================================================================
 
library(maaslin3)
library(phyloseq)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
 
# Load your saved phyloseq object
my_physeq <- readRDS("/Users/ediessy/Desktop/my_phyloseq.rds")
 
# -- Extract feature table and metadata -------------------------------------
feat_table <- as.data.frame(t(otu_table(my_physeq)))
meta       <- as.data.frame(sample_data(my_physeq))
 
# Force clean data.frame (fixes the xtfrm error)
meta <- data.frame(lapply(meta, function(x) {
  if (is.data.frame(x)) x[[1]] else x
}), row.names = rownames(meta))
 
# Add sequencing depth
meta$sequencing_depth <- sample_sums(my_physeq)
 
# -- Fixed covariates --------------------------------------------------------
SITE     <- "site_of_collection"   # <-- update to match your column name
SEQDEPTH <- "sequencing_depth"
 
# -- Define all primary outcomes to loop over --------------------------------
#    Update these to exactly match your metadata column names
OUTCOMES <- c(
  "chronic_rhinitis_12mo",
  "chronic_rhinitis_24mo",
  "chronic_rhinitis_36mo"
  # add more here as needed, e.g.:
  # "chronic_rhinitis_48mo",
  # "wheeze_12mo",
  # "atopy_36mo"
)
 
# -- Output root directory ---------------------------------------------------
OUTPUT_DIR <- "/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE"
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 1.  HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ===========================================================================
 
# Prepare a single outcome column (factor, drop NAs)
prepare_outcome <- function(meta, outcome_var) {
  meta[[outcome_var]] <- factor(meta[[outcome_var]])
  meta
}
 
# Run MaAsLiN3 for one outcome + normalization combo
run_maaslin <- function(feat_table, meta, outcome_var,
                        norm, transform, output_path,
                        median_comp, warn_prev) {
  maaslin3(
    input_data                   = feat_table,
    input_metadata               = meta,
    output                       = output_path,
    formula                      = paste0("~ ", outcome_var, " + ",
                                          SEQDEPTH, " + ", SITE),
    normalization                = norm,
    transform                    = transform,
    augment                      = TRUE,
    standardize                  = TRUE,
    max_significance             = 0.1,
    median_comparison_abundance  = median_comp,
    median_comparison_prevalence = FALSE,
    warn_prevalence              = warn_prev,
    save_models                  = FALSE
  )
}
 
# Extract results for the outcome variable only, tag with metadata
extract_results <- function(fit_out, outcome_var, norm_label) {
  abund <- fit_out$fit_data_abundance$results %>%
    filter(str_detect(metadata, fixed(outcome_var))) %>%
    mutate(model = "Abundance", norm = norm_label, outcome = outcome_var)
 
  prev <- fit_out$fit_data_prevalence$results %>%
    filter(str_detect(metadata, fixed(outcome_var))) %>%
    mutate(model = "Prevalence", norm = norm_label, outcome = outcome_var)
 
  bind_rows(abund, prev)
}
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 2.  MAIN LOOP
# ===========================================================================
 
# Master results list — one entry per outcome per norm method
all_results_list <- list()
 
for (outcome in OUTCOMES) {
 
  message("\n========================================")
  message("Processing outcome: ", outcome)
  message("========================================")
 
  # Prepare metadata for this outcome (factor + drop NAs for this outcome)
  meta_outcome <- prepare_outcome(meta, outcome)
 
  # Remove samples with NA for this outcome
  na_count <- sum(is.na(meta_outcome[[outcome]]))
  if (na_count > 0) {
    message("  Dropping ", na_count, " samples with NA for ", outcome)
    keep <- !is.na(meta_outcome[[outcome]])
    meta_outcome  <- meta_outcome[keep, , drop = FALSE]
    feat_sub      <- feat_table[rownames(meta_outcome), , drop = FALSE]
  } else {
    feat_sub <- feat_table
  }
 
  # ---- TSS + LOG -----------------------------------------------------------
  message("  Running TSS + LOG...")
  tss_output <- file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, paste0("maaslin3_TSS_", outcome))
 
  fit_TSS <- run_maaslin(
    feat_table  = feat_sub,
    meta        = meta_outcome,
    outcome_var = outcome,
    norm        = "TSS",
    transform   = "LOG",
    output_path = tss_output,
    median_comp = TRUE,
    warn_prev   = TRUE
  )
 
  res_TSS <- extract_results(fit_TSS, outcome, "TSS + LOG")
 
  # ---- CLR -----------------------------------------------------------------
  message("  Running CLR...")
  clr_output <- file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, paste0("maaslin3_CLR_", outcome))
 
  fit_CLR <- run_maaslin(
    feat_table  = feat_sub,
    meta        = meta_outcome,
    outcome_var = outcome,
    norm        = "CLR",
    transform   = "NONE",
    output_path = clr_output,
    median_comp = FALSE,
    warn_prev   = FALSE
  )
 
  res_CLR <- extract_results(fit_CLR, outcome, "CLR")
 
  # ---- Combine and store ---------------------------------------------------
  all_results_list[[outcome]] <- bind_rows(res_TSS, res_CLR)
 
  message("  Done: ", nrow(res_TSS), " TSS rows, ", nrow(res_CLR), " CLR rows")
}
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 3.  BUILD MASTER DATAFRAME
# ===========================================================================
 
master_results <- bind_rows(all_results_list)
 
# Reorder columns for readability
master_results <- master_results %>%
  select(outcome, norm, model, feature, metadata,
         coef, stderr,
         pval_individual, qval_individual,
         pval_joint, qval_joint,
         everything())
 
cat("\nMaster results dimensions:", nrow(master_results), "rows x",
    ncol(master_results), "cols\n")
 
# Quick summary of significant hits per outcome + norm
master_results %>%
  filter(qval_individual < 0.1) %>%
  count(outcome, norm, model) %>%
  print()
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 4.  CONCORDANCE TABLE (TSS vs CLR comparison per outcome)
# ===========================================================================
 
concordance_table <- master_results %>%
  filter(model == "Abundance") %>%
  select(outcome, norm, feature, coef, pval_individual, qval_individual) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = norm,
    values_from = c(coef, pval_individual, qval_individual),
    names_sep   = "_"
  ) %>%
  rename(
    coef_TSS  = `coef_TSS + LOG`,
    coef_CLR  = coef_CLR,
    pval_TSS  = `pval_individual_TSS + LOG`,
    pval_CLR  = pval_individual_CLR,
    qval_TSS  = `qval_individual_TSS + LOG`,
    qval_CLR  = qval_individual_CLR
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    concordant_direction = sign(coef_TSS) == sign(coef_CLR),
    CLR_nominal_sig      = pval_CLR < 0.05,
    confidence_tier      = case_when(
      concordant_direction & CLR_nominal_sig  ~ "High",
      concordant_direction & !CLR_nominal_sig ~ "Moderate",
      !concordant_direction                   ~ "Low"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(qval_TSS < 0.1) %>%   # keep only TSS-significant hits
  arrange(outcome, confidence_tier, qval_TSS)
 
cat("\nConcordance table for TSS-significant hits:\n")
print(concordance_table)
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 5.  SAVE TO DISK
# ===========================================================================
 
save_path <- file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "maaslin3_master_results")
dir.create(save_path, showWarnings = FALSE)
 
# Full master results (all outcomes, both norms, both models)
write.csv(master_results,
          file      = file.path(save_path, "master_results_all.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
 
# Significant hits only (qval_individual < 0.1)
write.csv(master_results %>% filter(qval_individual < 0.1),
          file      = file.path(save_path, "master_results_significant.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
 
# Concordance table
write.csv(concordance_table,
          file      = file.path(save_path, "concordance_TSS_vs_CLR.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
 
# R object (fastest to reload, preserves all data types)
saveRDS(master_results,
        file = file.path(save_path, "master_results.rds"))
 
cat("\nAll files saved to:", save_path, "\n")
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 6.  VOLCANO PLOTS — one per outcome, TSS abundance model
#     (expand as needed for CLR / prevalence)
# ===========================================================================
 
make_volcano <- function(df,
                         title       = "Volcano Plot",
                         q_threshold = 0.1,
                         top_n_label = 15) {
 
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      neg_log10_q = -log10(qval_individual + 1e-10),
      direction   = case_when(
        qval_individual < q_threshold & coef > 0 ~ "Up (significant)",
        qval_individual < q_threshold & coef < 0 ~ "Down (significant)",
        TRUE                                      ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      direction = factor(direction,
                         levels = c("Up (significant)",
                                    "Down (significant)",
                                    "Not significant"))
    )
 
  top_hits <- df %>%
    filter(qval_individual < q_threshold) %>%
    arrange(qval_individual) %>%
    slice_head(n = top_n_label)
 
  ggplot(df, aes(x = coef, y = neg_log10_q,
                 color = direction, label = feature)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(q_threshold),
               linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
               linetype = "solid", color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_text_repel(data         = top_hits,
                    size         = 2.8,
                    max.overlaps = 20,
                    box.padding  = 0.4,
                    segment.color = "grey50") +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Up (significant)"   = "#D73027",
                 "Down (significant)" = "#4575B4",
                 "Not significant"    = "grey70"),
      name = NULL
    ) +
    labs(title   = title,
         x       = "MaAsLiN3 coefficient (effect size)",
         y       = expression(-log[10](q[individual])),
         caption = paste0("q_individual < ", q_threshold, " (BH FDR)")) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
          legend.position = "bottom")
}
 
# Generate and save one volcano per outcome (TSS abundance)
for (outcome in OUTCOMES) {
 
  plot_data <- master_results %>%
    filter(outcome == !!outcome,
           norm  == "TSS + LOG",
           model == "Abundance")
 
  if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) next
 
  p <- make_volcano(plot_data,
                    title = paste0("TSS + LOG | Abundance\n", outcome))
 
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(save_path, paste0("volcano_TSS_", outcome, ".pdf")),
    plot     = p,
    width    = 8, height = 6
  )
}
 
cat("Volcano plots saved.\n")
 
 
###############################################################################
# TO ADD NEW OUTCOMES LATER:
#   Simply add the column name to the OUTCOMES vector at the top and re-run.
#   New results will be appended to the master dataframe automatically.
#
# TO RELOAD SAVED RESULTS WITHOUT RE-RUNNING MAASLIN3:
#   master_results <- readRDS(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR,
#                             "maaslin3_master_results/master_results.rds"))
###############################################################################
```
